data.title: Atour Lifestyle Holdings Non-GAAP EPS of $0.11, revenue of $262.63M beats by $2.4M
data.description:
• Atour Lifestyle Holdings press release (NASDAQ:ATAT): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.11.
  • Revenue of $262.63M beats by $2.4M.
  • As of March 31, 2025, there were 1,727 hotels with a total of 194,559 hotel rooms in operation across Atour’s hotel network, representing rapid increases of 32.6% and 31.3% year-over-year in terms of the number of hotels and hotel rooms, respectively.
  • As of March 31, 2025, there were 755 manachised hotels under development in our pipeline. The average daily room rate (“ADR”) was RMB418 for the first quarter of 2025, compared with RMB430 for the same period of 2024 and RMB420 for the previous quarter.
  • The occupancy rate was 70.2% for the first quarter of 2025, compared with 73.3% for the same period of 2024 and 77.0% for the previous quarter.
  • The revenue per available room (“RevPAR”) was RMB304 for the first quarter of 2025, compared with RMB328 for the same period of 2024 and RMB337 for the previous quarter.
  • For the full year of 2025, the Company currently expects total net revenues to increase by 25% to 30% compared with the full year of 2024.

data.title: Trump's 'big beautiful bill' advances to House floor
data.description:
President Donald Trump's "One Big Beautiful Bill" cleared a procedural vote late on Wednesday and is currently being debated by the House. After this, a vote may be held Thursday morning to pass the bill and send it to the Senate.

After a nearly 22-hour-long session, the House Rules Committee voted 8-4 overnight to advance the bill. House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-LA) said he is confident of the bill being passed as well as early as Thursday.

All House Democrats are expected to vote against the bill, and it's unclear if Johnson will get the support needed from his party to pass the measure.

The bill proposes about $3.8T in tax cuts (including the extension of Trump's 2017 tax cuts), and rollback of tax breaks for electric vehicles and green energy. It also seeks about $150B for the Pentagon, which would also cover Trump's mass deportation efforts and border security.

The most divisive proposals in the bill are the stricter requirements for Medicaid and the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), which provides food stamps to low-income Americans.

According to the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office, the proposed tax changes would increase the federal deficit by an estimated $3.8T over the next decade. It also noted that the tax cuts would benefit top earners more than low-income households.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Precious metals tick lower as dollar steadies, Brent crude falls 1.5%
data.description:
Precious metals and oil futures ticked lower on Thursday as the US dollar (DXY) held steady, while focus was also on the passage of a tax cut bill and trade negotiations.

U.S. crude futures (CL1:COM) and Brent futures (CO1:COM) fell 1.5% each to $60.67 and $63.95 a barrel, respectively.

OPEC+ members are reportedly discussing whether to agree on another super-sized production increase at their meeting on June 1, potentially making it the third consecutive month in which the group would add extra barrels to the market.

Meanwhile, gold prices (XAUUSD:CUR) eased 0.4% from a two-week peak, even as traders remained worried about the debt implications of the GOP budget bill favored by President Donald Trump.

Investors generally tend to lean toward the safe-haven asset amid economic and geopolitical concerns. 

The U.S. Treasury saw weak demand for its $16 billion sale of 20-year bonds on Wednesday, adding pressure to both the dollar and Wall Street, as markets remain on edge following Moody’s downgrade of the U.S. credit rating last week.

On the geopolitical front, Yemen’s Houthis said they launched two drones and a missile at multiple targets in Israel, the group’s military spokesperson said on X.

While the fifth round of nuclear talks between Iran and the United States is scheduled for May 23 in Rome, according to Oman’s foreign minister.

Among other precious metals, spot silver (XAGUSD:CUR) -0.59% to $33.25 an ounce, platinum (XPTUSD:CUR) lost 0.5% to $1,067.80, and palladium (XPDUSD:CUR) fell 1.7% $1,014.00.

ETFs liked to precious metals: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (GDX),(GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ),(SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Stock futures little changed with bond yields calling the shots
data.description:
Stock index futures were slightly up Thursday with attention still focused on Treasury yields.

S&P futures (SPX) +0.1% and Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.2% rose, while Dow futures (INDU) were close to the flatline.

Treasury yields were easing back slightly after the Wednesday spike. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was down 2 basis points to 4.58%. The long bond (US30Y) was steady at 5.09%.

Stocks sold off in the previous session and bonds sold off sharply, pushing yields higher, with a weak Treasury auction spooking traders already worried about the debt implications of the GOP budget bill favored by President Donald Trump.

"The most troubling part of the market reaction (to the auction) is that the dollar (DXY) is weakening at the same time," Deutsche Bank's George Saravelos said. "To us this is a clear signal of a foreign buyer's strike on US assets and the associated US fiscal risks we have been warning for some time."

"At the core of the problem is that foreign investors are simply no longer willing to finance US twin deficits at current level of prices."

"It is hard for US equities to stay resilient in this environment,"  Saravelos added. "The 2023-24 period saw a combined rise in US yields and equities as the market was revising US growth expectations higher. This was entirely reasonable. Today is very different."

"It is all a building fiscal risk premium in to US assets. It is hard to make the case that such a (negative) driver of the rising cost of capital is positive for risk assets."

House Republicans were still trying to pass the bill in the early hours of Thursday after working on a deal with fiscal hawks overnight.

"The details of the budget are constantly changing, so their effects are hard to judge, but the broad impact is to push the US further along a rising debt path," UBS' Paul Donovan said. "Bond investors are less than happy."

"Economically quite a lot of the budget is extending existing tax cuts, which does not add to growth. Removing taxes on tips might push inflation higher than reported. If employers reduce pay (included in consumer price data via prices) and encourage tipping (not included in consumer price data), the cost of living will rise more than reported inflation."

On the economic calendar, weekly jobless claims arrive before the bell, with economists expecting them to stay about steady at 230K. The May S&P Global flash PMI hits shortly after the start of trading.

Also after the open, April existing home sales figures arrive. The consensus is for a rise to an annual rate of 4.15M.

"The greater uncertainty and additional uptick in longterm interest rates accompanying the tariff shock so far will add to the headwinds facing housing at the margin," Pantheon Macro's Samuel Tombs said. "But the fundamental predicament for the housing market has been little changed since early 2023."

"Mortgage rates of around 7% are high enough to discourage many new buyers, while the big gap between market rates and the average rates on existing mortgages is encouraging many existing homeowners to stay put, also reducing the number of homes coming on the market and keeping prices relatively high."

data.title: Manchester United stock slips as club misses out on Europa League title
data.description:
Shares of Manchester United (NYSE:MANU) fell on Thursday after the club's final chance at silverware this season vanished with a 1-0 defeat to Tottenham Hotspur.

Manchester United manager Ruben Amorim has offered to quit the soccer giant without any compensation if the club wants to sack him after losing the Europa League final. 

While both Manchester United and Tottenham have had dreadful Premier League seasons, the winner of the Europa tournament automatically qualifies for the Champions League next season and will see a major financial windfall. 

Last year, publicly traded Manchester United (NYSE:MANU) generated $777.1 million in revenue, which was down from the prior two years.

A major consideration with the stock in the past has been the success of the iconic club in the Champion's League.

Shares of Manchester United (NYSE:MANU) fell over 7% in premarket trading, and are down 16% so far this year.

When it comes to Wall Street analysis, 2 out of 4 analysts recommend a Strong Buy, and 2 recommend a Hold. Seeking Alpha analysts have a Sell rating on the company.
